name: Mookwon Seo, PhD
summary: Results-driven Embedded Software Engineer with a PhD in Mathematics, 8+ years of experience developing real-time embedded control systems, motor control algorithms, and firmware for hardware interfaces. Expertise in C/C++, bare-metal programming, and PCB design, with a proven record of optimizing performance and enhancing reliability in embedded applications.

items.label: Programming Languages
items.value: Python, SQL, Julia, C/C++, C#.NET WPF, CUDA, MATLAB

items.label: Embedded Tools
items.value: IAR Embedded Workbench, STM32, ARM Cortex, Bare-metal firmware

items.label: Hardware
items.value: PCB Design (Altium Designer), Microcontrollers, Motor Controllers, Sensors

items.label: Modeling & Optimization
items.value: Mathematical Modeling, Optimization, Sequential Quadratic Programming (SQP), Parallel computing (GPU, multi-threading), Machine Learning, Scikit-Learn

items.label: Physics Simulation
items.value: Rigid body dynamics, 6 DOF simulation, Aerodynamics simulation, Fluid simulation

items.description: Modeling and simulation, data acquisition, and reconciliation for an innovative wind energy system; electronics design, PCB creation, and control logic development via bare-metal firmware.

roles.title: SOFTWARE ENGINEER | DATA SCIENTIST (2022 – Present)
bullets: Conducted structural fatigue and deformation analysis for turbine components, optimizing design performance using OpenFAST and OWENS simulation tools.
Designed high-performance algorithms for real-time physical simulations, ensuring industry-standard accuracy and computational efficiency.
Built and managed scalable data pipelines for 4+ TB of wind data using HDF5, streamlining storage and retrieval for large-scale analysis.

roles.title: EMBEDDED SOFTWARE ENGINEER (2018 – 2022)
bullets: Developed real-time motor control algorithms with Simulink and IAR Workbench, achieving a 20% increase in efficiency and 15% reduction in power consumption.
Engineered and debugged bare-metal firmware for ARM Cortex-based microcontrollers, ensuring high reliability for sensor integration and motor communication.
Performed deep-dive analysis of motor phase and sensor data, identifying key operational patterns that drove a 15% increase in system performance.

roles.title: MODELING AND SIMULATION ENGINEER (2016 – 2018)
bullets: Developed a custom GPU-accelerated Sequential Quadratic Programming (SQP) algorithm in C#, outperforming commercial tools like KNITRO through parallel processing.
Led the implementation of complex physical simulations incorporating hundreds of variables and constraints to maximize power output while minimizing costs.
Applied advanced mathematical modeling principles to optimize large-scale system performance and computational efficiency.

items.description: Optimized a hydrology model for the Upper Colorado Basin using high-performance computing techniques, resulting in a 30% reduction in processing time.

roles.title: RESEARCH ASSISTANT
bullets: Optimized a hydrology model for the Upper Colorado Basin using high-performance computing techniques, resulting in a 30% reduction in processing time.
Investigated and implemented advanced parallel processing algorithms to improve the scalability and efficiency of large-scale environmental models.

items.description: Developed numerical schemes for hyperbolic equations in plasma physics and topology optimization for future internet protocols.

roles.title: PROJECT RESEARCHER
bullets: Developed numerical schemes for hyperbolic equations in plasma physics and topology optimization for future internet protocols.
Presented research findings to peer experts, contributing to advancements in computational science and internet topologies.
